title: jacquemontia eastwoodiana
intro: Jacquemontia eastwoodiana is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family, native to the island of Guam and the northern Mariana Islands. It is also known as Eastwood's morning glory or Eastwood's bindweed. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In traditional herbal preparations, Jacquemontia eastwoodiana is often made into a tea or a topical ointment to take advantage of its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
